mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the discount rate applied to future earnings and eases financial conditions, which raises S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is confirmed when policy rates are declining from already-low levels and fails if rate cuts coincide with equity weakness from recession fears.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% and still near the effective lower bound
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 60% of months when FEDFUNDS is falling or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in RSAFS above trend indicates stronger consumer spending and revenue growth, which should lift near-term equity returns as analysts revise sales expectations upward. The effect is strongest when retail sales acceleration is broad rather than one-off and fails if higher sales are offset by margin compression or demand exhaustion.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises above its 3-month moving average after two consecutive monthly increases
failure_condition: Reject if triggered RSAFS expansions do not lead to positive 20-day SPX returns at least 55% of the time, or if the signal's 3-year rolling IC is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE signals weakening labor income and higher recession probability, which should depress equity prices over the next month through lower expected consumption and earnings. The mechanism is most reliable when unemployment is rising from an already elevated level and fails if markets have already fully priced the deterioration.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of unemployment-spike episodes or if unemployment rises are not followed by below-median returns within 20 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in INDPRO above its recent trend indicates improving industrial output and capex momentum, which supports cyclical earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal fails when production gains are driven by inventory restocking without final demand support.
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly-like 60-day forward returns are not above baseline after INDPRO trend improvements, or if the rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of rising HOUST and rising GDPC1 signals a broad demand-led expansion in housing and output, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. This interaction matters because housing strength alone can be misleading unless real activity is also firm; the signal fails when GDP is flat or revised lower.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either proxy alone and if triggered joint-expansion periods do not deliver positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL acceleration stays elevated while FEDFUNDS remains very low, real rates become more negative and liquidity conditions improve for risk assets, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal fails if inflation is being re-priced as growth-negative rather than liquidity-positive.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds 3% while FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction does not produce positive 20-day returns relative to baseline, or if high-inflation/low-rate episodes show negative SPX returns in most cases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies a labor-market break that should weigh on equity prices quickly through lower expected household income and weaker margins. The effect is strongest in recession transition windows and fails when job losses are offset by strong productivity or monetary easing.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE rises at least 0.2 percentage points over the same span
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon SPX returns are not negative in at least 60% of triggered episodes or if the signal loses significance after controlling for each labor series separately.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp slowdown in GDP growth combined with weak retail sales indicates demand recession, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations are marked down. The mechanism is regime-conditional and is most informative after an earlier expansion phase; it fails if GDP weakness is already offset by aggressive policy support.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day SPX returns are not below baseline when both GDP growth and retail sales weaken, or if the joint signal underperforms single-series effects over a 3-year rolling window.
